title: Bolbostemma biglandulosum
intro: Bolbostemma biglandulosum, also known as Tian Hua Fen, is a type of medicinal plant belonging to the Cucurbitaceae family. It has been used in traditional Chinese medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties. The bioactive constituents of Bolbostemma biglandulosum include saponins, alkaloids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. Traditionally, Bolbostemma biglandulosum is made into herbal preparations such as decoctions, infusions, and capsules, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, and is also used in modern medicine for its immunomodulatory and anti-cancer activities.
taxonomy: Bolbostemma biglandulosum is a plant species that belongs to the family Cucurbitaceae. This family comprises a wide variety of plants commonly known as gourds, melons, or cucumbers. The genus Bolbostemma is a relatively small group within the Cucurbitaceae family, primarily found in certain parts of Asia. The exact classification of Bolbostemma biglandulosum within its genus can vary depending on the source, but it is generally accepted as a distinct species.
